title: Chapter 4 Quiz: Phishing Dynamics
description: Test your knowledge of email spoofing, phishing content creation, landing pages, and advanced MFA bypass techniques.

questions.question: What percentage of cyber attacks are estimated to begin with a phishing email?
questions.explanation: Over 90% of breaches start with a phishing email. It's the primary initial access vector.

questions.question: Which email authentication method tells receiving servers what to do (quarantine or reject) if SPF or DKIM checks fail?
questions.explanation: DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting & Conformance) defines the policy (`p=reject` or `p=quarantine`).

questions.question: Display Name Spoofing is effective because mobile email clients often hide the actual sender email address.
questions.explanation: True. On a small screen, users see 'PayPal Support' but not the `@paypal-fake.com` address.

questions.question: You receive an email with the sender name 'IT Help Desk' but the actual address is 'support@acmecorp-support.com'. What technique is this?
questions.explanation: It's a lookalike domain (`acmecorp-support.com`). The attacker owns this domain, so SPF/DKIM passes.

questions.question: The email header that allows an attacker to redirect replies to a malicious address is called ______.
questions.explanation: The 'Reply-To' header overrides the default reply address. Users think they are replying to the legitimate sender.

questions.question: Which Cialdini weapon is most directly triggered by a subject line like 'ACTION REQUIRED: Your password expires in 2 hours'?
questions.explanation: Scarcity (limited time). The user feels they must act now or lose access.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of including a fake 'Unsubscribe' link in a phishing email footer?
options: To add legitimacy and bypass spam filters
To trick the user into unsubscribing
To track if the email was opened
questions.explanation: Legitimate marketing emails have unsubscribe links. Their presence reduces suspicion and improves deliverability scores.

questions.question: It is better to embed images directly in a phishing email as attachments rather than hosting them on a remote server.
questions.explanation: False. Embedded attachments increase file size and spam score. Hosting images remotely (or using legitimate CDN links) is stealthier.

questions.question: When cloning a login page for credential harvesting, which HTML element must be modified to send data to the attacker's server?
questions.explanation: The 'action' attribute of the `<form>` tag tells the browser where to POST the username and password.

questions.question: Why is an SSL certificate (HTTPS) essential for a modern phishing landing page?
options: It prevents the 'Not Secure' warning in the browser
It makes the site load faster
questions.explanation: Without HTTPS, Chrome shows a glaring red warning. With HTTPS (even from Let's Encrypt), the user sees the green lock icon and feels safe.

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'Redirect Chain' or hiding the phishing page behind a long random URL path (e.g., `/a7f3k9m2/login`)?
options: To make the link look more secure
To evade automated security crawlers (Google Safe Browsing)
To track which email campaign the user came from
questions.explanation: Crawlers scan the root of domains. By hiding the phish deep, you prevent bots from flagging your domain.

questions.question: Adversary-in-the-Middle (AitM) phishing tools like Evilginx steal the user's password directly from the login form.
questions.explanation: False. They steal the **authenticated session cookie** after the user successfully logs in and completes MFA. The password passes through but the cookie is the real prize.

questions.question: An attacker repeatedly attempts to log in with a valid password, causing the user's phone to receive dozens of MFA push notifications. The attacker then calls pretending to be IT support. This attack is called:
questions.explanation: MFA Fatigue exploits the user's annoyance and desire to stop the notifications, tricking them into approving a fraudulent login.

questions.question: Why is QR Code Phishing (Quishing) effective at bypassing corporate security controls?
options: Users scan the code with their personal phone, bypassing corporate web filters
QR codes are too small for antivirus to scan
questions.explanation: The user's personal mobile device is outside the corporate perimeter. No URL filtering, no endpoint detection.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a recommended technique for improving phishing email deliverability?
options: Warming up the sending IP address
Using a valid SPF record for the sending domain
Sending 10,000 emails simultaneously from a new domain
questions.explanation: Sending a massive blast from a brand new domain is a major spam signal. Volume should be increased gradually (warming).

questions.question: The tool commonly used to automate credential harvesting with a cloned website in Kali Linux is the ______.
questions.explanation: SET has a dedicated 'Credential Harvester' module for cloning sites and capturing POST data.

questions.question: What is the 'Golden Ticket' in the context of an Evilginx attack?
questions.explanation: The session cookie allows the attacker to impersonate the user without needing a password or MFA prompt.

questions.question: Smishing (SMS Phishing) is generally less effective than email phishing because people are more suspicious of text messages.
questions.explanation: False. SMS open rates are over 98%. People trust text messages more than email and have fewer defenses on their phones.

questions.question: You are writing a spear phishing email. According to the lesson, what is the optimal placement for the malicious link?
options: At the very bottom of the email after a long story
In the middle of a paragraph of text
questions.explanation: The user should not have to scroll. The call to action must be immediately visible.

questions.question: What is the purpose of geofencing a phishing landing page?
options: To track where the victim is located
To only serve the phishing content to IP addresses in the target country, reducing exposure to scanners
To display a localized version of the login page
questions.explanation: By showing a 404 to IPs outside the target region (e.g., Russia, China), you reduce the chance of security researchers or automated crawlers flagging your site.

questions.question: Using a URL shortener like Bitly is a common technique to hide a malicious domain in an SMS phishing message.
questions.explanation: True. The user can't see the destination until they click, and some SMS gateways block known bad domains but allow shorteners.

questions.question: Which of the following is a 'Whaling' attack?
options: Sending a fake Amazon reset email to 10,000 people
Sending a fake legal subpoena to the CEO
Sending a fake invoice to the Accounts Payable department
Leaving infected USB drives in the parking lot
questions.explanation: Whaling targets high-value executives (C-Suite). Spear phishing targets specific departments or individuals.

questions.question: In a Red Team phishing campaign, what is the most important document to have BEFORE sending any emails?
options: The signed Scope of Work (SOW) and Authorization Letter
A backup of the phishing server
questions.explanation: Without explicit written authorization, phishing is a federal crime. The SOW defines exactly who can be targeted.

questions.question: The practice of slowly increasing email sending volume from a new domain to build a positive reputation with email providers is called ______.
questions.explanation: Warming the IP/domain prevents immediate spam classification.

questions.question: An email contains a link that says 'https://www.microsoft.com'. When you hover over it, the actual link is 'https://www.micr0soft.com/login'. What is this technique called?
questions.explanation: The displayed text (anchor text) differs from the actual `href` link. This is a classic phishing technique.
